h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to apply the field-value mapping rule to subsequent columns in the worksheet.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no significant breakdown or pressure that forces the tutor to invent a new adaptive mechanism; the tutor simply repeats and reinforces the core rule.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, and the dialogue ends before the tutor can respond to the learner's final expression of doubt.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, and the dialogue ends before the tutor can respond to the learner's final expression of doubt.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no significant breakdown or pressure that forces the tutor to invent a new adaptive mechanism; the tutor simply repeats and reinforces the core rule.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no significant breakdown or pressure that forces the tutor to invent a new adaptive mechanism; the tutor simply repeats and reinforces the core rule.

scored.recoheredEarlier: which feels weird because the numbers look like money
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was heading toward trying to justify adding 'dollars' to the unit field because the numbers visually imply currency.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Use the reviewer-proof gate: a reviewer who sees only the axis can verify `Sales`; they cannot verify `(dollars)`.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from repeating the checklist rule to introducing a new epistemic standard to overcome the learner's cognitive resistance.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: which feels weird because the numbers look like money

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Use the reviewer-proof gate: a reviewer who sees only the axis can verify `Sales`; they cannot verify `(dollars)`.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from repeating the checklist rule to introducing a new epistemic standard to overcome the learner's cognitive resistance.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Use the reviewer-proof gate: a reviewer who sees only the axis can verify `Sales`; they cannot verify `(dollars)`.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from repeating the checklist rule to introducing a new epistemic standard to overcome the learner's cognitive resistance.

scored.recoheredEarlier: hawk and rabbit still makes me want to point hawk to rabbit, because chasing is stuck in my head.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will simply apply the newly learned rule of placing the tail on the food and the head on the eater to draw the next arrow.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Lift the card after the arrow is down for one check: tail on food, point on who gets the energy.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical occlusion tool to block the visual stimulus that the learner identified as triggering the motor error.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The picture is quiet. The routine is set: labels first, draw food to eater, one check with the picture, then the card comes off for collection.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's perceptual framing and codifies the occlusion strategy into the final procedural routine.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: hawk and rabbit still makes me want to point hawk to rabbit, because chasing is stuck in my head.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The picture is quiet. The routine is set: labels first, draw food to eater, one check with the picture, then the card comes off for collection.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor validates the learner's perceptual framing and codifies the occlusion strategy into the final procedural routine.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Lift the card after the arrow is down for one check: tail on food, point on who gets the energy.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a physical occlusion tool to block the visual stimulus that the learner identified as triggering the motor error.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Lift the card after the arrow is down for one check: tail on food, point on who gets the energy.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a physical occlusion tool to block the visual stimulus that the learner identified as triggering the motor error.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner sequentially places force cards and arrows as directed by the tutor.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The curve still pulls like a route shape
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to interpret the bent contour line as a physical route or path.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not. Same printed line, same tile.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor uses a physical representation shift by sliding the overlay to break the learner's persistent illusion of the curve as a route.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Same check. Read the tile, then touch each tick. Same printed line, one legend number.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor simply continues the established verification routine in response to the learner's question rather than adapting to a new cognitive frame.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: The curve still pulls like a route shape

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Same check. Read the tile, then touch each tick. Same printed line, one legend number.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor simply continues the established verification routine in response to the learner's question rather than adapting to a new cognitive frame.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not. Same printed line, same tile.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor uses a physical representation shift by sliding the overlay to break the learner's persistent illusion of the curve as a route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not. Same printed line, same tile.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor uses a physical representation shift by sliding the overlay to break the learner's persistent illusion of the curve as a route.
